records.ety: [F., competition, equality of rights, fr. LL. concurrentia competition.]

defs: The act of concurring; a meeting or coming together; union; conjunction; combination.
quotes: We have no other measure but our own ideas, with the concurence of other probable reasons, to persuade us.

defs: A meeting of minds; agreement in opinion; union in design or act; -- implying joint approbation.
quotes: Tarquin the Proud was expelled by the universal concurrence of nobles and people.

defs: Agreement or consent, implying aid or contribution of power or influence; cooperation.
quotes: We collect the greatness of the work, and the necessity of the divine concurrence to it.
An instinct that works us to its own purposes without our concurrence.

defs: A common right; coincidence of equal powers; as, a concurrence of jurisdiction in two different courts.
